identificationModule.briefTitle: A Study of LY900014 Formulation at Different Injection Sites in Healthy Participants
identificationModule.officialTitle: Effect of Injection Site on the Relative and Absolute Bioavailability of Single Dose of LY900014 in Healthy Subjects

descriptionModule.briefSummary:
This study evaluates a new formulation of insulin lispro, LY900014, a drug that lowers blood sugar. It is administered by injection into the vein and under the skin of the abdomen, thigh and arm.

The study will be conducted in healthy people to investigate the effect of different injection sites on the amount of insulin lispro in the bloodstream.

Side effects and tolerability will be documented. The study will last for about 10 weeks for each participant, including screening and follow up. Screening is required within 28 days prior to entering the study.

armGroups.label: LY900014 (SC Abdomen)
armGroups.description: Single dose of 15-U of LY900014 administered subcutaneously (SC) into the abdomen in one period

armGroups.label: LY900014 (SC Thigh)
armGroups.description: Single dose of 15-U of LY900014 administered SC into the thigh in one period

armGroups.label: LY900014 (SC Arm)
armGroups.description: Single dose of 15-U of LY900014 administered SC into the arm (deltoid) in one period

armGroups.label: LY900014 (IV)
armGroups.description: Single dose of 15-U of LY900014 administered intravenously (IV) in one period

primaryOutcomes.measure: Pharmacokinetics: Insulin Lispro Area Under the Concentration Versus Time Curve (AUC) Following LY900014 Administration
primaryOutcomes.description: Pharmacokinetics(PK): Insulin lispro AUC from time zero to 10 hours post dose \[AUC(0-10h)\].

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Total Amount of Glucose Infused (Gtot) Over Duration of the Clamp Procedure
secondaryOutcomes.description: Glucodynamics: Gtot is the total glucose infusion over the clamp duration (10 hours) and is used to measure the study drug action over time as measured by the euglycemic clamp procedure. During the euglycemic clamp procedure, blood glucose concentrations are held constant after the administration of LY900014 by adjusting the exogenous glucose infusion rate.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Every 10 minutes for 30 minutes predose, every 2.5 minutes for 30 minutes, then every 5 minutes for 120 minutes, then every 10 minutes for 120 to 480 minutes and every 10 minutes for 480 to 600 minutes post-dose

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Overtly healthy male or a female (not pregnant and agreeable to take birth control measures until study completion)
* Have a body mass index (BMI) of 18 to 30 kilogram per square meter (kg/m²)
* Have normal blood pressure, pulse rate, electrocardiogram (ECG), blood and urine laboratory test results that are acceptable for the study
* Are nonsmokers, have not smoked for at least 6 months prior to entering the study

Exclusion Criteria:

* Are currently participating in or completed a clinical trial within the last 30 days or any other type of medical research judged to be incompatible with this study
* Have previously participated or withdrawn from this study
* Had blood loss of more than 450 milliliters (mL) within the last 3 months

references.citation: Leohr JK, Dellva MA, LaBell E, Coutant DE, Linnebjerg H. Evaluation of the Pharmacokinetic Profile of Ultra Rapid Lispro Administered Subcutaneously at Different Injection Sites. Clin Ther. 2022 Jun;44(6):836-847. doi: 10.1016/j.clinthera.2022.04.001. Epub 2022 May 13.

participantFlowModule.preAssignmentDetails: This is an open-label, randomized, crossover, up to 10-hour euglycemic clamp study in which participants were randomized into one of four treatment sequences. Participants received single doses of 15 units of LY900014 on 4 occasions (treatment Periods 1 through 4) with a wash-out period of at least 3 days between study drug administration.

groups.title: LY900014 Treatment Sequence 1: Abdomen,Thigh, Deltoid(Arm), IV
groups.description: Single dose of 15-U of LY900014 administered subcutaneously (SC) into the abdomen in Period 1, Thigh in Period 2, Deltoid (Arm) in Period 3, and single 15-U intravenous (IV) bolus injection in Period 4.

groups.title: LY900014 Treatment Sequence 2: Deltoid, Abdomen, IV, Thigh
groups.description: Single dose of 15-U of LY900014 administered SC into the: Deltoid in Period 1, Abdomen in Period 2, single 15-U IV bolus injection in Period 3, and single 15-U SC administration into the Thigh in Period 4.

groups.title: LY900014 Treatment Sequence 3: IV, Deltoid, Thigh, Abdomen
groups.description: Single 15-U IV bolus injection of LY900014 administered in Period 1, single 15-U dose administered SC in the Deltoid in Period 2, Thigh in Period 3, and Abdomen in Period 4.

groups.title: LY900014 Treatment Sequence 4: Thigh, IV, Abdomen, Deltoid
groups.description: Single dose of 15-U of LY900014 administered SC in Thigh in Period 1, single 15-U IV bolus injection in Period 2, single dose of 15-U administered SC in the Abdomen in Period 3, and Deltoid in Period 4.

milestones.type: Received at Least One Dose of Study Drug

groups.title: Overall
groups.description: Single 15-U dose of LY900014 administered in treatment Period 1- 4, as SC injection into the abdomen, the thigh, the deltoid or an IV bolus injection, per randomized treatment sequence.

categories.title: Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

analyses.groupDescription: Geometric Least Squares Means (LSMeans) were controlled for injection site, study period, and sequence as fixed effects, and subject within sequence as a random effect.
analyses.paramType: Ratio of Geometric Least Squares Means

analyses.groupDescription: Geometric LSMeans were controlled for injection site, study period, and sequence as fixed effects, and subject within sequence as a random effect.

outcomeMeasures.title: Total Amount of Glucose Infused (Gtot) Over Duration of the Clamp Procedure
outcomeMeasures.description: Glucodynamics: Gtot is the total glucose infusion over the clamp duration (10 hours) and is used to measure the study drug action over time as measured by the euglycemic clamp procedure. During the euglycemic clamp procedure, blood glucose concentrations are held constant after the administration of LY900014 by adjusting the exogenous glucose infusion rate.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: All participants who have received at least one dose of study drug administered and have completed at least one clamp procedure. The IV treatment arm was needed only for PK as it served as the reference to determine the absolute bioavailability.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Every 10 minutes for 30 minutes predose, every 2.5 minutes for 30 minutes, then every 5 minutes for 120 minutes, then every 10 minutes for 120 to 480 minutes and every 10 minutes for 480 to 600 minutes post-dose
